title: 'Neither I nor Italy ever beg': Meloni hits back at Trump after his photo remark
content: Giorgia Meloni, the Italian Prime Minister, firmly dismissed Donald Trump's allegations that she asked him for a photo at the G7 summit, calling his statements 'entirely fictitious.' Expressing her surprise and disappointment, Meloni stated that neither she nor her country would ever resort to begging. This confrontation signifies a potential rift in the previously friendly relationship between Meloni and Trump.

title: Israel, Hezbollah agree to ceasefire after mediation by Qatar, US and Iran
content: A ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah is scheduled to commence on Friday afternoon, marking a significant pause in months of hostilities along the border. With mediation efforts from Qatar, the US, and Iran, this agreement seeks to ease tensions and avert a larger conflict in the region. Although neither party has commented publicly, there is cautious optimism for peace and stability in areas affected by the turmoil.

title: 'Hope Speaker gives us justice': Abhishek files disqualification pleas against rebel MPs
content: Expressing hope that Lok Sabha Om Birla​​​​​​ would "act in accordance with the Constitution," Banerjee said: "The Lok Sabha Speaker is the custodian of the House, not the protector of the government of the day. Every citizen, especially elected representatives, must work within the ambit of the law. Therefore, one has to function according to the provisions laid down in the Constitution."

title: 'If not for Samson, would India have won?': Sreesanth takes aim at Gambhir
content: Former pacer S Sreesanth has questioned head coach Gautam Gambhir's significant contribution to India's recent white-ball triumphs, emphasizing the crucial roles played by players like Sanju Samson and Suryakumar Yadav. Sreesanth argued that on-field leaders and players made key decisions, contrasting this with a more involved coach like Ashish Nehra. He also linked India's Test struggles to Gambhir's tenure, advocating for a mentor over a coach.

title: After DOJ, Pentagon’s AI chief sides with Elon Musk's xAI in datacenter pollution case
content: The US Department of Justice is intervening in a lawsuit against Elon Musk's xAI, citing national security concerns. Pentagon AI chief Cameron Stanley testified that xAI's Grok AI is crucial for military operations, including targeting, and has been used to deploy thousands of munitions. He stated that halting xAI's operations could impair national security capabilities and put the US at a disadvantage against adversaries.

title: Agartala-bound IndiGo flight hit by lightning at Kolkata airport
content: An IndiGo aircraft at Kolkata airport was struck by lightning on Friday during a severe thunderstorm. While no passengers were harmed, two ground personnel were briefly affected and received medical evaluation. The flight was later operated by a different aircraft.

title: One of world’s richest persons & Louis Vuitton CEO on Elon Musk becoming trillionaire
content: Elon Musk has become the world's first trillionaire following SpaceX's historic public listing, with the company's valuation exceeding $2 trillion. Bernard Arnault, CEO of LVMH, acknowledged Musk's dominance in the rocket industry but clarified that the wealth represents company value, not liquid assets. Musk emphasized SpaceX's ambition to make humanity multi-planetary and space travel accessible to all.

title: 'It is not good for KL Rahul': Ex-India captain questions Gambhir's strategy
content: India has secured the ODI series against Afghanistan, using the matches to test new player combinations. While Ishan Kishan and Shubman Gill impressed at number three, former captain Krishnamachari Srikkanth questioned KL Rahul's deployment in a lower batting order, advocating for him to bat higher. Srikkanth also raised concerns about player fatigue due to the demanding schedule.

title: 'We waited 500 years, now wait 15 days more': CM Yogi on Ram Temple fund probe
content: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath announced a Special Investigation Team (SIT) to probe allegations of financial irregularities in Ram Mandir offerings. He urged devotees and political parties to avoid baseless comments until the investigation concludes, assuring that truth will be separated from falsehood.

title: Exclusive: Yuvraj Singh to take IPL coaching plunge with Delhi Capitals
content: Former India cricketer Yuvraj Singh is set to join the Delhi Capitals support staff from the 2027 IPL season. This marks his first formal coaching role in the league, building on his extensive mentorship of young players. His hands-on approach and impact on careers has been considered to be highly valued.

title: Meghan Markle's bond with Taylor Swift shifts amid Travis Kelce wedding
content: The preparations for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s high profile wedding are in full swing as the two are reportedly set to marry on 3rd July after dating for close to three years. While there have been many rumors about who the two will be inviting to their wedding, it seems Meghan Markle is not happy about it.

title: Dak Prescott gives health update amid Sarah Jane Ramos breakup drama
content: Dak Prescott, the Dallas Cowboys’ star player, recently went through a pretty painful break up with his ex fiancee, Sarah Jane Ramos, after their wedding in April was called off just weeks prior in March sparking panic among many. While there have been allegations about the popular quarterback cheating on his ex fiancee, it seems Dak Prescott was pretty stressed about the situation.

title: 'A very tough cookie': Trump says PM Modi a world leader 'he admires'
content: US President Donald Trump has lauded Prime Minister Narendra Modi as "a very tough cookie" and one of his most admired global leaders, alongside China's Xi Jinping. Speaking to Axios, Trump highlighted their strong personal rapport, even amidst trade tensions. This praise follows their recent G7 meeting, where Trump emphasized the close India-US relationship and PM Modi's formidable negotiating skills, suggesting a trade deal is nearing completion.

title: E20 petrol attracts ants? BPCL responds to viral claim, explains matter
content: Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited (BPCL) has officially clarified that E20 petrol does not attract ants, dismissing viral social media claims. The company stated there is no scientific evidence to support the notion, explaining that the ethanol used in fuel is processed to remove sugars and contains insect-repellent denaturants.

title: Red Tape ordered to pay Rs 8,000 for charging customer Rs 10 for carry bag
content: A consumer commission in Rohtak has ordered footwear brand Red Tape to refund Rs 10 for a carry bag and pay Rs 8,000 in compensation and costs. The commission ruled that charging for the bag constituted a deficiency in service and an unfair trade practice. The brand's own affidavit admitted the charge was unnecessary and unjustified, leading to the penalty.

title: JD Vance reveals his mother's shocking question about Usha Vance's Indian origin
content: US Vice President JD Vance shared a touching anecdote about his wife Usha and his late grandmother, Mamaw, on Steven Bartlett's podcast. Vance expressed regret that his grandmother never met Usha, noting their shared intelligence and strong will, despite vastly different backgrounds. He also recounted his mother's initial, culturally unaware question about Usha's 'tribe,' highlighting his family's unfamiliarity with diverse cultures, though they eventually embraced Usha wholeheartedly.

title: In days before he killed house help, friends saw marked change in doctor
content: South Delhi residents are baffled by the arrest of dermatologist Manish Gupta for allegedly murdering his domestic help. While neighbours saw him as reserved, friends noted behavioral changes and recalled he may have been treated for depression. Gupta was known for his charitable work and professional achievements, making the accusation of violence against a long-time employee difficult to comprehend.

title: The silent threat below: Why Indian Navy needs many more ASW helicopters
content: India's naval security faces a growing submarine threat from Pakistan and China. To counter this, the Indian Navy urgently needs to expand its anti-submarine warfare helicopter fleet. While new MH-60R helicopters have been acquired, more are essential for protecting vast maritime interests. Accelerating indigenous helicopter programs is crucial to ensure frontline warships have integral airborne ASW capabilities, closing a critical gap as underwater threats escalate across the Indo-Pacific.

title: Delhi HC postpones hearing on Salman Khan’s plea against ‘Kala Hiran’ release
content: Salman Khan's plea against the 'Kala Hiran' film makers, alleging violation of his personality rights, saw its Delhi High Court hearing postponed. The filmmakers sought more time to respond, while Khan's lawyer pushed for immediate interim relief, citing unauthorized use of the actor's life story and image for commercial gain. The court directed Khan's team to provide documents to the respondents, with the next hearing set for July 1.

title: The great reverse migration: Why India's professionals are leaving Metro cities
content: A fascinating trend is emerging as Indian professionals increasingly desert sprawling metropolises in favor of tier-2 and tier-3 cities, searching for a richer quality of life. The rising costs of living, extensive travel for work, and the stress of city life are significant forces behind this migration.

title: 'All of Lebanon must burn!' Israel strikes kill 18 after death of four soldiers
content: Intensified Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon overnight killed at least 18 people, despite a recent Iran-US agreement for a ceasefire. The violence coincides with the postponement of crucial US-Iran technical talks in Switzerland, raising concerns about the regional peace process. Israel claims strikes targeted Hezbollah infrastructure following soldier deaths.

title: As Accenture shares fall 20%, Julie Sweet says investors are missing the point
content: Accenture's stock plummeted nearly 20% following fiscal third-quarter results that missed revenue expectations and showed a decline in new bookings. Despite the drop, CEO Julie Sweet urged investors to remain patient, highlighting the company's strong positioning for long-term growth driven by AI adoption and a revised salary structure to offer immediate cash to employees.

title: Man thrashes wife, tonsures her head; arrested after video surfaces
content: A shocking incident unfolded in Korea district, Chhattisgarh, where a man was arrested for brutally assaulting his wife. He allegedly tonsured her head and forced her to drink urine. The FIR was registered after disturbing videos surfaced online. The woman, who had a love marriage 15 years ago, accused her husband of character assassination and abandonment prior to the attack.

title: 'Virat Kohli will play for at least three-four more years': RCB CEO Rajesh Menon
content: Virat Kohli delivered another strong IPL season, finishing as RCB’s top run-scorer in IPL 2026 with 675 runs at a strike rate of 165.84 - his highest-ever in a single edition. In the final, he played a key unbeaten 75-run knock that helped RCB become only the third team after Chennai Super Kings and Mumbai Indians to successfully defend their title.

title: 'Shameless, ungrateful, & corrupt': Aaditya Thackeray on 6 MPs as party faces split 2.0
content: In his first reaction to the crossover of six of the nine Lok Sabha MPs, who constitute two-thirds of the party's strength in the Lower House, Thackeray Junior said: "Maharashtra won't tolerate this". Calling those who are expected to switch to the Eknath Shinde camp "shameless, ungrateful, and corrupt individuals", the ex-Maharashtra minister said the MPs "shamelessly" sold themselves, staking their "reputation and family names".

title: The big guns: How India's atillery push could make it a firepower superpower
content: India's artillery is undergoing a significant overhaul, aligning with the global shift towards long-range precision warfare. This modernization, driven by the Field Artillery Rationalisation Plan, aims to replace older guns with advanced indigenous systems.

title: Biden aide funded Wuhan lab to 'manipulate' Covid probes? Gabbard's big claim
content: Former intelligence director Tulsi Gabbard released documents alleging Dr. Anthony Fauci funded gain-of-function research in Wuhan and misled Congress. The documents reportedly show Fauci's role in directing US taxpayer funds and interacting with intelligence officials to suppress lab-leak origins.  These claims emerged during a declassification review, with whistleblowers alleging retaliation.

title: India, Russia jointly developing small, hypersonic BrahMos variants
content: India and Russia are jointly developing advanced BrahMos missile variants, including smaller, lighter, and hypersonic versions. These next-generation missiles will significantly boost India's strike capabilities, offering enhanced precision, reduced costs, and integration with a wider range of platforms like fighter jets and unmanned systems.

title: 'Chor, chor': Eggs thrown at TMC leader Vijay Singh outside West Bengal court
content: TMC leader Vijay Singh faced egg pelting and shouts of "chor, chor" outside Asansol court. This follows a similar incident where former minister Udayan Guha was egged outside Dinhata Police Station after his arrest in an alleged extortion case. BJP cited political violence during Guha's tenure. Guha expressed ignorance about his arrest's reason.

title: Ashirvad Sooryavanshi: Batting all-rounder, fish lover and a 'tabahi' in the making
content: Another youngster from the Sooryavanshi household is beginning to attract attention. Ten-year-old Ashirvad Sooryavanshi, who started playing cricket only six months ago, smashed 103 off 87 balls in his first match for Cricket Academy Tajpur. A right-handed batting all-rounder and fish lover, Ashirvad trains on the same pitches where elder brother Vaibhav honed his skills and dreams of watching him score a century in the UK.

title: World's first drone AWACS takes flight: What MQ-9B's new radar means for India
content: Sweden's Saab has teamed up with General Atomics from the US to achieve a major milestone: the successful testing of the MQ-9B drone featuring cutting-edge early warning radar technology. This development could redefine aerial surveillance operations, making it more accessible for countries, particularly India, which face vast border and maritime security challenges.

title: PM Modi’s gifts to French, Slovak leaders showcase India’s cultural heritage
content: Prime Minister Modi presented leaders in France and Slovakia with exquisite Indian heritage gifts. French President Macron received a Kalamkari Mahabharata painting, while Brigitte Macron was gifted a Pochampally stole. Slovak officials were honored with Kashmiri carpets, Dokra art, Himroo textiles, and ancient Ayurvedic texts, showcasing India's rich craftsmanship and intellectual legacy.

title: India's golden treasure: Andhra Pradesh to become country's largest top gold producer
content: Andhra Pradesh is set to become India's leading gold supplier, with significant reserves identified in Kurnool district. Jonnagiri village alone is estimated to hold 50 tonnes of gold. Commercial production has commenced, and further exploration is expected to boost reserves. The state aims to leverage private investment for this substantial mining endeavor.

title: Musk tops Buffett in a day: 7 stats on this week’s number platter
content: Elon Musk's wealth surge highlights a future arriving early.  China's consumer spending shows a wobble, while Indian tech jobs face a squeeze.  However, blue-collar roles are growing, and civil engineering sees a comeback.  India's universities are rising, but China leads.  Meanwhile, India's subsidy bill has tripled, reflecting a complex economic landscape where the future is here, but not for everyone.

title: NDA bags 19 out of 26: How close is the ruling coalition to Rajya Sabha two-third majority
content: The BJP-led NDA has strengthened its Rajya Sabha presence, winning 19 of 26 recent seats to reach 150 members. This brings the alliance closer to a two-thirds majority, with potential further gains from upcoming bypolls and shifts in opposition alliances. However, regional parties and upcoming Uttar Pradesh elections could still influence the balance of power.

title: Mukesh Ambani's Jio files IPO papers; offers 27 crore shares of Rs 10 each
content: According to the DRHP, approximately Rs 27,500 crore from the issue proceeds has been earmarked for the full or partial repayment of borrowings undertaken by the company.

title: Black rain, smoke over Moscow: Russia-Ukraine trade bombs, drones and missiles as war intensifies
content: Ukraine launched a significant drone attack on a major Moscow oil refinery, causing a large fire and disrupting airport operations. This strike, occurring after pledges of Western support, underscores Kyiv's strategy to pressure Russia's energy infrastructure and escalate the conflict's domestic impact.

title: Renting a house? Ask these 10 questions before signing the agreement
content: Finding a rental home is thrilling, but missing important details before you sign can lead to unexpected expenses. It's vital for renters to inquire about all costs beyond the monthly rent, grasp security deposit rules, and know who will take care of maintenance issues.

title: Vision 2036 unveiled in Kerala revised budget
content: The budget has unveiled an ambitious project to train sportspersons from the State for the 2036 Olympics

title: A.P. High Court adjourns hearing on IPS officer Sunil Naik’s bail plea to July 3
content: Extending him interim protection till that date, the court asks the officer, accused of involvement in the alleged custodial torture of former YSRCP MP Raghu Ramakrishna Raju, to cooperate in the investigation

title: Lokesh invites West Bengal investors to become partners in A.P.'s development journey
content: At a session at Kolkata on Investment Opportunities in A.P., the IT and HRD Minister says the State is leading in the implementation of reforms and able to mobilise 25% of the total investments coming into India; he meets heads of various companies and suggests opportunities available in various sectors across Andhra Pradesh

title: Educated fraudsters increasing in society, says Veerappa
content: As many as 123 public grievances were taken up during the hearing, and officials were directed to resolve several long-pending issues within stipulated deadlines

title: State govt. allocates ₹50 crore for cultural park in memory of M.T.
content: The proposed park is expected to come up as a multidisciplinary cultural space featuring venues for various art forms

title: Railways decides to set up help desks at stations to help candidates travelling to NEET centres
content: Tiruchi Railway Division plans to set up help desks at important stations spanning across several districts such as Tiruvannamalai, Puducherry, Karaikal, Vellore and Villupuram

title: Centre to deploy over 100 officers for VB-G RAM G rollout from July 1
content: The officers will act as facilitators and resource persons during the rollout phase, working with State governments, district administrations and field-level functionaries to support implementation, strengthen capacities, share best practices and address operational challenges, says Ministry

title: Kerala Budget: ‘One Kerala Karuthal Mission’ announced
content: The initiative aims at helping people who are facing serious financial hardships and are unable to afford critical medical care or finance higher education of their children

title: Coalition government earned public trust through welfare and development initiatives, says Minister
content: The State government has fulfilled several of its key election promises and is now focusing on infrastructure and economic growth, says Mandipalli Ramprasad Reddy at the district-level celebration of “Two Years of Trust – Development and Welfare” in Chittoor

title: Kerala Budget: Budget promises zero-tolerance on crimes against women and children, provides thrust for fight against drug abuse and proliferation
content: Confidential toll-free helplines and a portal will be established for reporting crimes against children. Scheme titled ‘Makalkkoppam’ will be implemented to prevent crimes against women

title: Kasaragod gets special focus in revised Budget
content: The Budget states that the special development packages being implemented in the backward districts of Kasaragod, Wayanad, and Idukki will be accelerated

title: Roads around schools fail safety test as Hyderabad records 128 accidents involving minors this year
content: Accorrding to a study 80% of schools did not have school-zone warning boards, 90% lacked visible speed-limit signage and 70% of schools lacked designated pick-up and drop-off zones, 60% had no footpaths

title: SIT probe into Ram temple fund row will bring truth to light: Adityanath
content: Adityanath encouraged anyone with evidence related to the case to present it to the SIT, and warned that anyone found guilty, regardless of their position, would not be spared

title: SIT starts probe into Ram Mandir donations issue, reaches Ram temple complex
content: The State government constituted the three-member SIT at the request of the Shree Ram Teerth Kshetra Trust

title: 20 petitions submitted seeking disqualification of rebel MPs: TMC's Abhishek Banerjee
content: Twenty people met the Speaker and claimed they should be treated as a separate group. Later, we got to know that those MPs claimed to have joined another party, NCPI; nobody has heard the name of this group, says Trinamool Congress leader Abhishek Banerjee

title: Justice on hold: India’s courts are clogged
content: Civil cases in district courts face the longest delays, exposing a stark mismatch between caseload and capacity

title: Supreme Court declares right to walk on footpath a fundamental right, wants law
content: Apex court says walking powered the freedom struggle, politics and social reforms, but is yet to be recognised as a fundamental right; asks government to frame a law to protect pedestrians

title: Key transport infra projects find no mention in Kerala Budget
content: There is no mention of the proposed high-speed rail project, Sabari Rail, and the Balaramapuram–Vizhinjam underground rail. An amount of ₹100 crore has been allotted for the Balaramapuram–Navaikulam Ring Road project and ₹20 crore for Light Metro projects. A sum of ₹200 crore has been earmarked for the preliminary activities of a proposed aviation and logistics hub

title: CM orders crackdown on misuse of guarantee schemes after AG report flags issues
content: According to the report, around ₹60 crore was disbursed through nearly three lakh instalments into accounts linked to multiple beneficiaries

title: Kerala Budget: major tourism and traditional industry push for Alappuzha
content: At the heart of the Budget is an ambitious plan to establish a Southern Economic Corridor linking Thiruvananthapuram, Kollam, and Alappuzha, while positioning the district as the country’s Blue Economy Capital

title: Kerala Budget: Kottayam skywalk returns to centre stage as UDF clears revival plan
content: The announcement fulfils a key electoral promise made by Kottayam MLA and Speaker Thiruvanchoor Radhakrishnan, who had pledged to prioritise the project once the UDF returned to power

title: Kerala Budget earmarks ₹50 crore for establishing Tribal University in Wayanad
content: The proposed Tribal University and Indigenous Knowledge Zone is envisaged as a centre for higher learning, research, innovation, and entrepreneurship among tribal communities

title: ‘Savukku’ Shankar moves Madras High Court seeking CBI probe against former Chennai Police Commissioner A. Arun
content: Says the Vigilance Commissioner failed to take action against the officer despite adverse findings and observations made by the court against him on May 29, 2026

title: Kerala Revised Budget allocates ₹243.80 crore for forest protection; steps intensified to tackle human-wildlife conflict
content: Key measures include a scientific census of tigers and leopards, the deployment of rapid response teams, and the use of modern technology to mitigate conflicts. It also states that Kerala will be made a completely carbon-neutral State by 2050.

title: Medical colleges asked not to grant leave to students during NEET-UG re-exam period
content: The instruction has been issued in compliance with directions received from the Department of Higher Education under the Ministry of Education regarding the conduct of the medical entrance examination, says National Medical Commission

title: Dr. YSR Horticultural University unveils AI learning platform
content: Vice-Chancellor says AI-driven learning systems can enhance educational outcomes and access to quality content

title: Priyank Kharge’s remark on RSS invokes the question of legal personality
content: The Karnataka Home Minister has asked about the registration, organisation structure, funding and donations and also about the overall functioning of the RSS

title: High Court orders notices to Mamata, Abhishek in contempt case over TMC Martyrs' Day rally
content: The petitioner's lawyer, Srikanta Dutta, moved a division bench presided by Justice Arijit Banerjee, alleging contempt of the court's 2018 order, directing that gatherings on thoroughfares must not cause total blockage of the road

title: Pink Line elevated stretch by August 2026, full corridor by March 2027: Krishna Byre Gowda
content: The Pink Line metro project has witnessed repeated delays, missing multiple deadlines, drawing criticism from commuters. The corridor is expected to significantly ease north–south connectivity once operational.

title: ₹75,000-crore projects to create 1.5 lakh jobs in Visakhapatnam: Collector
content: At a two-year celebration, the District Collector said Visakhapatnam was drawing nearly half the State’s investment and ₹75,000-crore projects would bring 1.5 lakh jobs

title: Here are the big stories from Karnataka today
content: Welcome to the Karnataka Today newsletter, your guide from The Hindu on the major news stories to follow today. Curated and written by Nalme Nachiyar.

title: Andhra Pradesh SSC supplementary results: pass rate climbs to 82.39%, says Nara Lokesh
content: The Minister says 78,261 of 94,990 students passed the May exams, crediting a 20-day coaching plan held after the SSC public examinations

title: Trump Asked Which World Leader He Admires Most, He Picks PM Modi, Xi Jinping
content: Donald Trump made the remarks on "The Axios Show" on Thursday, shortly after returning from a G7 summit in France that he described as "very dominant".

title: Delhi Gets First Dedicated Women's Police Station: How It Will Work
content: The specialised unit in the Subzi Mandi area that will exclusively investigate crimes against women and children across the North District became operational on June 19 and has jurisdiction over the entire North District.

title: Forest Department Employee Among 2 Arrested In J&K For Sheltering Terrorists
content: According to police, preliminary investigation indicates the duo played a pivotal role in facilitating the movement of local terror operatives, arranging clandestine hideouts and extending material assistance to terrorists traversing the upper reaches of Dachhan.

title: Walking On Demarcated Footpaths A Fundamental Right: Supreme Court
content: In a judgment delivered on Thursday, a bench comprising Justice PS Narasimha and Justice AS Chandurkar declared that the right to walk forms part of the freedoms guaranteed under Article 19(1)(d) of the Constitution.

title: India's Monsoon Paradox: Flood Alert For Northeast While Delhi Sizzles
content: Satellite imagery released by the IMD on Friday showed a vast belt of lightning activity stretching from the Himalayan region through eastern and central India to parts of the south, highlighting the volatile weather conditions unfolding across multiple states simultaneously.

title: Uddhav Sena Rebel's Daughter Signals Loyalty To Thackerays
content: This deepening factional divide has mirrored itself uniquely within the Patil household, leading to an internal strategic split.

title: China Begins Building World's Largest Dam, India Plans Counter In Arunachal Pradesh
content: India is now trying to accelerate its own strategic response while closely monitoring Beijing's every move on the contested river.

title: Ratings Pressure, Israel Rift: Why Trump Signed 'Unequal' Iran Peace Deal
content: As sanctions begin to ease and economic pressure on Iran diminishes, the next phase of negotiations will determine whether Washington can secure meaningful and lasting nuclear restrictions or whether Tehran emerges with even greater bargaining power.

title: 18 Dead In Israeli Strikes On Lebanon As US-Iran Talks Pushed Back
content: Lebanon's state-run National News Agency reported at least 16 people were killed in Israeli airstrikes, which the Israeli military said were ongoing.

title: "It Could Change In A Week": Expert Explains Why US-Iran Deal Is Tricky
content: For Trump, the details of the peace agreement are politically embarrassing because they suggest a softer approach toward Iran than the administration previously promoted.

title: Ukraine's New Drone Strategy Is Putting Russian Supply Lines Under Pressure
content: Ukraine's latest drone campaign is focused on disrupting the flow of fuel, equipment and personnel behind Russian lines.

title: Hyderabad Court Summons Actor Allu Arjun In Pushpa 2 Stampede Case On June 22
content: Following the charge sheet, filed on December 24, the court issued summons to all 23 accused, including Allu Arjun, directing them to appear before it on June 22.

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex Slides 600 Points As Accenture Drags IT Stocks
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Oil prices edge higher after JD Vance threatened Israel against violating ceasefire.

title: Why Trump Thinks He's 'Most Powerful Person To Walk This Planet'
content: The post, written by a ‘Dave King’, compared Trump’s influence with some of history’s most well-known rulers and military leaders, including William the Conqueror, Genghis Khan, Napoleon and Alexander the Great.

title: A Rare Handshake At LoC Between Officers As Pak National Returns Home
content: Amid ongoing hostility between the two countries, a major rank officer of Indian Army was seen shaking hands with a Pakistan army officer, a rare moment.

title: Top US Scientist Funded Wuhan Lab That Sparked Covid? A Big 'Expose'
content: Tulsi Gabbard, who last month quit as Trump's intelligence chief, said Anthony Fauci funded "dangerous gain-of-function research on bat coronaviruses" at the Wuhan Institute of Virology.

title: US-Iran LIVE Updates: JD Vance Cancels Switzerland Visit, Muted Activity In Hormuz
content: US-Iran War LIVE Updates: Netanyahu pledged that Iran would never be allowed to acquire nuclear weapons, regardless of the terms of any agreement.

title: 'Cross-voting' in Karnataka MLC polls: 'Upset' top brass summons BJP state leadership
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. title: The chokepoint doctrine: How the Iran war exposed the rise of middle powers
content: john avlon: how long the united states and Iran have given themselves to negotiate a final deal? he says the answer is not ideology, not a nuclear deterrent, not even military strength . he asks: what is a middle power? middle powers do not rise through military supremacy or ideological appeal .

content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds. Join us as we craft knowledge with precision and passion, bringing you on a journey where the mysteries of the universe unfold with every word. Read More
